cases.input: I have uploaded a CT scan for a prostate cancer patient. The scan was acquired with 3mm slice thickness and includes the pelvis from the iliac crests to the ischial tuberosities. Please analyze the image quality, identify the prostate gland, and assess whether the scan quality is sufficient for brachytherapy planning.
cases.description: CT analysis test: image quality slice thickness prostate identification

cases.input: This CT scan shows a patient with cervical cancer. I need you to evaluate the tumor extent visible on CT, identify the tandem and ovoid applicator that was placed under anesthesia, and assess the applicator position relative to the cervix and uterus. Please provide measurements of the applicator geometry.
cases.description: CT analysis test: cervical tumor applicator position geometry measurement

cases.input: I uploaded a CT scan of a patient scheduled for prostate seed implant. The scan was done in the lithotomy position with a ultrasound probe in place. Please identify the prostate, seminal vesicles, bladder, and rectum, and provide approximate volumes for each structure.
cases.description: CT analysis test: prostate lithotomy anatomy segmentation volume

cases.input: Please analyze this head and neck CT scan for a patient with oral tongue carcinoma. I need to assess the extent of the primary tumor, evaluate lymph node involvement, and determine if there is mandibular invasion visible on CT.
cases.description: CT analysis test: oral tongue tumor lymph node mandible invasion

cases.input: This is a chest CT scan for a patient with endobronchial tumor who may be a candidate for endoluminal brachytherapy. Please evaluate the tumor location, degree of luminal obstruction, proximity to the carina, and identify any atelectasis or post-obstructive changes.
cases.description: CT analysis test: endobronchial tumor carina obstruction atelectasis

cases.input: I have uploaded a post-implant CT scan for a prostate brachytherapy patient. The scan was taken 30 days after I-125 seed implantation. Please analyze the seed distribution, identify any seed migration to the lungs, and provide an assessment of overall implant quality.
cases.description: CT analysis test: post-implant seed distribution migration lung quality

cases.input: Please examine this CT scan of a rectal cancer patient. The patient has a T3N1 rectal adenocarcinoma located 5cm from the anal verge. Assess the relationship of the tumor to the mesorectal fascia, evaluate for extramural vascular invasion, and determine the distance from the tumor to the anal sphincter.
cases.description: CT analysis test: rectal T3N1 mesorectal fascia EMVI anal sphincter

cases.input: This CT scan shows a breast cancer patient being considered for APBI. The lumpectomy cavity is visible in the upper outer quadrant of the left breast. Please identify the cavity, measure its dimensions, assess the chest wall distance, and evaluate the skin-to-cavity distance for balloon placement feasibility.
cases.description: CT analysis test: breast lumpectomy cavity APBI balloon feasibility chest wall

cases.input: I have a CT scan for a patient with recurrent endometrial cancer at the vaginal cuff. The CT shows some soft tissue thickening at the cuff. Please analyze the extent of the recurrence, evaluate the relationship to the bladder and rectum, and assess whether brachytherapy is feasible.
cases.description: CT analysis test: vaginal cuff recurrence endometrial bladder rectum feasibility

cases.input: Please analyze this CT scan for a patient with a penile cancer. The scan shows an exophytic lesion on the glans penis. Evaluate the depth of invasion, assess for inguinal lymph node involvement, and determine if the tumor is amenable to organ-preserving brachytherapy.
cases.description: CT analysis test: penile cancer depth invasion inguinal lymph node organ preservation

cases.input: This CT scan is from a patient with biliary tract cancer. I need to evaluate the tumor extent along the bile duct, assess vascular involvement of the portal vein and hepatic artery, and determine if interstitial brachytherapy can be safely performed.
cases.description: CT analysis test: biliary tract tumor portal vein hepatic artery interstitial

cases.input: I have uploaded a CT scan for a patient with a vulvar melanoma. The primary lesion is on the right labium majus. Please evaluate the tumor dimensions, depth of invasion, assess for groin lymph node metastases, and determine if interstitial brachytherapy is appropriate for this patient.
cases.description: CT analysis test: vulvar melanoma depth invasion groin lymph node interstitial

cases.input: Please analyze this CT scan of a pediatric patient with a bladder rhabdomyosarcoma. The patient is 4 years old. Evaluate the tumor extent, assess bladder wall invasion, evaluate for hydronephrosis, and determine if brachytherapy boost is indicated alongside external beam.
cases.description: CT analysis test: pediatric bladder rhabdomyosarcoma hydronephrosis brachytherapy boost

cases.input: This CT scan shows a patient with advanced cervical cancer being evaluated for brachytherapy. There is a large cervical mass with parametrial extension. Please assess the tumor volume, evaluate the relationship to the pelvic sidewall, and measure the distance to the ureters.
cases.description: CT analysis test: cervical cancer parametrial pelvic sidewall ureter volume

cases.input: I have uploaded a CT scan for a prostate cancer patient with a large prostate volume of approximately 80cc. Evaluate the prostate size, assess for median lobe protrusion into the bladder, and discuss whether this patient is suitable for brachytherapy or if neoadjuvant hormonal therapy should be considered.
cases.description: CT analysis test: prostate large volume median lobe neoadjuvant hormonal therapy

cases.input: Please analyze this CT scan of a patient with a skin cancer on the nose. The lesion is a 2cm squamous cell carcinoma. Evaluate the depth of invasion on CT, assess cartilage involvement, and determine if surface brachytherapy with a custom mold is feasible.
cases.description: CT analysis test: skin cancer nasal depth cartilage surface brachytherapy

cases.input: This CT scan is for a patient with esophageal cancer at the gastroesophageal junction. Evaluate the tumor length, assess the degree of luminal narrowing, evaluate for mediastinal lymphadenopathy, and determine if endoluminal brachytherapy boost is appropriate.
cases.description: CT analysis test: esophageal tumor length luminal narrowing mediastinal endoluminal

cases.input: I uploaded a CT scan showing a patient with liver metastases from colorectal cancer. There are two lesions in the right hepatic lobe. Please segment the lesions, measure their volumes and positions relative to the hepatic vasculature, and assess whether image-guided brachytherapy is feasible.
cases.description: CT analysis test: liver metastases segmentation volume hepatic vasculature feasibility

cases.input: Please analyze this CT scan for a patient with an anal canal cancer. The tumor is located at the dentate line. Evaluate the tumor extent, assess involvement of the internal and external sphincters, and determine if brachytherapy boost is indicated after external beam radiation.
cases.description: CT analysis test: anal canal dentate line sphincter brachytherapy boost

cases.input: This CT scan shows a patient with head and neck cancer requiring interstitial brachytherapy. There is a recurrent tumor in the floor of mouth. Please evaluate the tumor dimensions, assess mandibular proximity, evaluate the sublingual gland position, and determine optimal needle placement zones.
cases.description: CT analysis test: floor of mouth tumor mandible sublingual needle placement

cases.input: I have a CT scan for a female patient with a vaginal melanoma. The tumor is in the upper third of the vagina. Please evaluate the tumor extent, assess the relationship to the bladder and rectum, evaluate for lymph node involvement in the pelvis, and determine brachytherapy feasibility.
cases.description: CT analysis test: vaginal melanoma bladder rectum pelvic nodes feasibility

cases.input: This is a post-implant CT scan taken at day 1 for a prostate brachytherapy patient. Please analyze the seed positions, evaluate edema by comparing with the pre-implant prostate volume, and provide an edema-corrected dosimetric assessment.
cases.description: CT analysis test: post-implant day 1 seed edema dosimetry correction

cases.input: Please analyze this CT scan for a patient with a recurrent tumor at the anastomosis after rectal cancer surgery. The recurrence is at the colorectal anastomosis. Evaluate the tumor volume, assess invasion into surrounding pelvic structures, and determine if interstitial brachytherapy can be safely delivered.
cases.description: CT analysis test: anastomotic recurrence rectal cancer interstitial pelvic

cases.input: This CT scan is for a patient with a urethral cancer. Please evaluate the tumor extent along the urethra, assess bladder involvement, evaluate for pelvic lymph node metastases, and determine if brachytherapy is appropriate for this rare malignancy.
cases.description: CT analysis test: urethral cancer extent bladder pelvic lymph node brachytherapy

cases.input: I have uploaded a CT scan for quality assurance purposes. This is a CTCAE phantom scan with known dimensions. Please verify the geometric accuracy of the CT by measuring the phantom dimensions and comparing them to the known specifications to validate spatial calibration.
cases.description: CT analysis test: CTCAE phantom geometric accuracy spatial calibration QA

cases.input: I am evaluating PET-CT for brachytherapy planning in cervical cancer. FDG-PET can visualize the primary tumor and lymph nodes. How should I integrate PET findings into brachytherapy target volume delineation? What are the limitations of PET for brachytherapy planning? How does PET-CT compare with MRI for target delineation? Should I use PET-CT routinely for brachytherapy planning?
cases.description: Question about PET-CT integration in brachytherapy planning

cases.input: I am performing a quality assurance check on the CT simulation for brachytherapy planning. I need to verify: (1) CT number accuracy using a phantom, (2) geometric accuracy of the CT images, (3) the effect of contrast material on CT-based dose calculation, and (4) the appropriate CT-to-electron-density conversion table for brachytherapy applicators. How do I perform each of these QA checks?
cases.description: QA question for CT simulation in brachytherapy planning

cases.input: I need to understand how cone-beam CT (CBCT) can be used for brachytherapy guidance. What are the advantages of CBCT over conventional CT for brachytherapy? How does CBCT image quality compare to CT for applicator and target visualization? What are the radiation dose implications of using CBCT for brachytherapy guidance? Should I use CBCT routinely for brachytherapy fraction imaging?
cases.description: Question about cone-beam CT for brachytherapy guidance

cases.input: I am evaluating the use of MRI for post-implant dosimetry evaluation of prostate LDR brachytherapy. How does MRI improve seed visualization compared to CT? What MRI sequences are best for seed localization? How does MRI-based post-implant dosimetry compare with CT-based evaluation? What are the practical challenges of MRI-based post-implant dosimetry?
cases.description: Question about MRI for post-implant dosimetry evaluation

cases.input: I need to understand how to handle MRI artifacts in brachytherapy planning. What types of MRI artifacts can affect brachytherapy planning (susceptibility artifacts from metallic applicators, chemical shift artifacts, motion artifacts)? How do these artifacts affect target and applicator delineation? What correction methods exist? How should I adjust the MRI protocol to minimize artifacts?
cases.description: Technical question about MRI artifacts in brachytherapy planning

cases.input: I am evaluating the use of artificial intelligence (AI) for brachytherapy planning. How can AI assist in: (1) automatic applicator reconstruction from CT/MRI, (2) automatic target volume delineation, (3) dose optimization, and (4) plan quality evaluation? What are the current limitations of AI in brachytherapy? What validation studies exist for AI-based brachytherapy planning?
cases.description: Question about AI applications in brachytherapy planning

cases.input: I am evaluating the use of MRI for brachytherapy planning in rectal cancer. How does MRI help in delineating the rectal tumor and mesorectal fascia? What MRI sequences are recommended for rectal cancer brachytherapy? How does MRI-based planning compare with CT-based planning for rectal brachytherapy? What are the challenges of MRI-guided rectal brachytherapy?
cases.description: Question about MRI for rectal cancer brachytherapy planning

cases.input: I am evaluating the accuracy of 3D ultrasound for brachytherapy guidance in gynecologic cancer. How does 3D ultrasound compare with MRI for target delineation? What are the advantages of 3D ultrasound over 2D ultrasound? Can 3D ultrasound replace MRI for brachytherapy planning in resource-limited settings? What are the training requirements for 3D ultrasound-guided brachytherapy?
cases.description: Question about 3D ultrasound for brachytherapy guidance

cases.input: I need to understand the role of artificial intelligence in automatic organ-at-risk delineation for brachytherapy. How accurate are current AI algorithms for OAR segmentation in the pelvis? What are the clinical requirements for AI-based OAR delineation? How should I validate AI-generated contours before using them in brachytherapy planning? What are the current limitations?
cases.description: Question about AI for automatic OAR delineation in brachytherapy

cases.input: I am evaluating the use of spectral CT for brachytherapy planning. How does spectral CT improve material characterization compared to conventional CT? Can spectral CT reduce artifacts from brachytherapy applicators? What are the advantages for dose calculation? What are the current limitations?
cases.description: Question about spectral CT for brachytherapy planning

cases.input: I am evaluating the use of emerging imaging technologies for brachytherapy guidance. Are there any novel imaging modalities that could improve brachytherapy planning? What are the current research directions in brachytherapy imaging? How might photoacoustic imaging or optical coherence tomography be applied to brachytherapy? What is the timeline for clinical implementation?
cases.description: Forward-looking question about emerging imaging technologies for brachytherapy
